Fix outpost founding failing in wilderness

townId=null and worldX/worldY set. foundPlayerOutpost() now:

  to avoid placing on water/mountains

commit.message:
fix: migrate to BAP/PP API scopes to resolve AADSTS65002 in corporate tenants (#11)

## Problem

The extension was requesting the
`https://service.flow.microsoft.com/user_impersonation` scope. In
corporate tenants where user consent is disabled by policy, this causes
an **AADSTS65002** preauthorization error, completely blocking sign-in
for enterprise developers.

## Solution

Switch to Microsoft first-party API scopes that are pre-authorized in
all Microsoft Entra tenants:

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `https://api.bap.microsoft.com/.default` | Environment listing (BAP
API) |
| `https://api.powerplatform.com/.default` | Cloud flows & flow runs
(Power Platform API) |

This is the same pattern used by Microsoft's own [Power Platform Tools
VS Code
extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=microsoft-IsvExpTools.powerplatform-vscode).
These scopes do not require admin consent or app registration.

## Changes

### `src/auth/AuthProvider.ts`
- Removes the single `service.flow.microsoft.com` scope
- Adds `getBapToken()` and `getPpApiToken()` — separate tokens for the
two APIs
- Maps sovereign cloud API base URLs to their corresponding BAP/PP API
scopes
- Exposes `bapBaseUrl` and `ppApiBaseUrl` getters for the client to use
- `signIn()`: fixed `forceNewSession` → `createIfNone` (avoids forcing
re-auth when already signed in)

### `src/api/PowerAutomateClient.ts`
- `listEnvironments`: migrated to BAP API (`api.bap.microsoft.com`)
- `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`: migrated to Power Platform API
(`api.powerplatform.com`)
- Methods not yet supported by the PP API now throw a clear "not yet
available" error (handled by the `registerTools.ts` catch wrapper —
Copilot Chat will see a user-friendly message)

### `package.json`
- Default `apiBaseUrl` updated to `https://api.powerplatform.com`
- Sovereign cloud enum values updated to PP API URLs

### `docs/azure-app-registration.md` & `README.md`
- Docs rewritten to reflect no app registration requirement
- Removes stale `service.flow.microsoft.com` references

## Temporarily reduced API surface

Some API methods now throw "not yet available" until the Power Platform
API achieves parity with the legacy `service.flow.microsoft.com`
endpoints. These are clearly documented in the client. Affected
operations:
- `listConnections`, `createOrUpdateFlow`, `setFlowState`,
`addFlowToSolution`
- `getFlowRunActions`, `getFlowRunActionInputsOutputs`
- `resubmitFlowRun`, `cancelFlowRun`, `getFlowHttpSchema`,
`getFlowTriggerUrl`, `triggerFlow`

**Working:** `listEnvironments`, `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`

## Supersedes

Closes and supersedes #10 — this PR solves the same corporate-tenant
auth problem at the root cause (wrong scope) rather than adding an Azure
CLI workaround.

## Testing

- [ ] Sign in with a personal Microsoft account
- [ ] Sign in with a corporate account in a tenant with user consent
disabled
- [ ] Environments list loads correctly
- [ ] Flows list loads for an environment
- [ ] Flow runs load for a selected flow
- [ ] Unavailable tools return a clear error message in Copilot Chat

---------

commit.message:
fix(task-116): force-push repair branch on reruns; fix clipped_text edge case (#147)

Two edge cases surfaced during the live TASK-116 repair-lane smoke tests
that the earlier follow-up commits didn't fully close.

## Changes

- **Force-push repair branch on reruns** — `cmd_finalize` now uses `git
push --force -u origin` instead of a plain push. When a run successfully
pushes the repair branch but then fails to create the PR (as happened in
run `24164768702`), a subsequent forced rerun rebuilds the local branch
from `FETCH_HEAD` and the plain push is rejected because the remote
already has diverged history. Repair branches are automation-owned so
force push is safe.

  ```python
  # before
  git("push", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  # after
  git("push", "--force", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  ```

- **`clipped_text` edge case** — when `limit <= len(ellipsis)`, now
returns `normalized[:limit]` (plain truncation) instead of
`ellipsis[:limit]`, which would emit a meaningless `".."` or `"."`.
Unreachable with the current `MAX_REPLACEMENT_SUMMARY_CHARS = 2500`
caller, but the guard is now semantically correct.

  ```python
  # before
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return ellipsis[:limit]   # e.g. ".." or "." — confusing
  # after
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return normalized[:limit]  # plain truncation
  ```

---------

commit.message:
feat(copilot): persist SDK session ID across app restarts

Store copilot_session_id and copilot_session_agent in SQLite so Copilot
SDK sessions survive app restarts, mirroring the existing sdk_session_id
pattern used by the Claude provider.

On first turn per session per app run, copilot.ts seeds the in-memory
copilotSessionIds map from the DB (guarded by a seededFromDb Set so the
DB is only hit once). After createSession/resumeSession, both the session
ID and the active agent are written back to the DB. On agent change or
resumeSession failure the DB columns are NULLed so a stale ID is never
reused on the next restart.

Fix ConfigurationBinder failing to bind empty array to constructor parameter (#126470)

Fix #126312

When binding an empty JSON array (`[]`) to a constructor parameter of
type `string[]`, `ConfigurationBinder` threw:

```
ArgumentException: Object of type 'System.String' cannot be converted to type 'System.String[]'.
```

## Root Cause

In `BindParameter`, the `BindingPoint` was initialized with
`config.GetSection(parameterName).Value` — the config section's string
value. For an empty JSON array `[]`, this value is `""` (empty string).
This caused `BindInstance` to skip the empty-collection creation code
path, which requires `bindingPoint.Value` to be `null`. The empty string
value was then returned as the parameter value, causing the
`ArgumentException` when the constructor was invoked via reflection.

For comparison, property binding correctly initializes the
`BindingPoint` with `property.GetValue(instance)` (typically `null` for
uninitialized arrays), so the empty-collection path works.

## Fix

Remove the `initialValue` argument from the `BindingPoint` constructor
in `BindParameter`, so it defaults to `null`. This is correct because:

- Constructor parameters have no "existing value" to preserve
- The config section's value is already read inside `BindInstance` from
the `config` parameter directly — passing it as `initialValue` was
redundant
- For simple types (string, int, etc.), `TryConvertValue` succeeds and
overwrites the initial value regardless

## Test

Added `TestBindingEmptyArrayToConstructorParameter` — reproduces the
exact scenario from the issue (empty JSON array bound to a `string[]`
constructor parameter with a null default).

the interpreter during measurement.

hot path.

commit.message:
fix: prevent callee JIT deopt from propagating to caller

When a callee's Maglev or baseline JIT code deopts, the caller should
NOT be forced to deopt as well. Instead, the callee deopt should be
handled by marking the deopt, invalidating the JIT cache, and falling
through to the interpreter.

Fix call_undefined_receiver0_inner:
- Maglev path: on callee deopt, mark maglev deopted, invalidate cache,
  restore context, and fall through to baseline/interpreter.
- Baseline path: on callee deopt, mark baseline deopted, restore
  context, and fall through to interpreter.

Fix Maglev codegen (emit_direct_call_0/1/2):
- After JIT call returns JIT_DEOPT, instead of deopting the caller via
  emit_deopt_check_rax, fall back to the runtime stub which runs the
  interpreter and returns a valid result.
- Mono-hit path: reload callee from cache and call runtime stub.
- Cache-miss path: pop callee from stack and call runtime stub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: repair stocked products by updating stock entries before product QU fields

The old approach (bridging conversions) failed because Grocy rejects QU
conversions referencing deleted units. New approach:
1. When product QU update fails due to stock constraint, fetch stock entries
2. Update each stock entry's qu_id to the new valid unit
3. Retry the product QU update (now succeeds because stock uses new QU)
4. Fall back to fixing non-stock fields individually if still failing

Also adds get_stock_entries() and update_stock_entry() to Grocy API client.
469 tests passing.

Auto-detects podman or docker. Reads OPENHAB_URL and OPENHAB_TOKEN
from environment or .env.local file. Stops any existing container
with the same name before starting a fresh one.

Usage:
  OPENHAB_URL=https://... OPENHAB_TOKEN=oh.... ./scripts/run-local.sh
  # or create .env.local with those vars

Instead of dropping messages received while paused, buffer them.

commit.message:
refactor: soft pause — keep WebSocket open, suppress sends/receives

Instead of closing the WebSocket on pause (requiring reconnect on
resume), just stop the auto-sync timer and set _paused flag.
syncNow() and handleSocketMessage() already check the flag.
resume() restarts the timer and immediately syncs pending edits.
No socket teardown/reconnect needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix MCP mount: set streamable_http_path='/' so endpoint is /mcp not /mcp/mcp

Mounting at '/' intercepted all routes (/healthz, /readyz, /tools) causing
404s. Instead, keep mount at '/mcp' but configure the SDK internal path to
'/' so the composed path is /mcp.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: use sentinel package version

Keep package manifests at 0.0.0 on main so releases are versioned only by the release workflow.

Use npm version --no-git-tag-version in CI so both package.json and package-lock.json are stamped to the computed release tag during publish.
